name: rolepod-dblab
description: Data-layer safety + truth verifier for AI agents — 5 shipped skills (db-introspect, db-query, db-explain, db-migrate-verify, db-write) + MCP server with 5 rolepod_db_* tools. Works standalone with a connection string; pair with the `rolepod` parent plugin (detected via the marker file `<git-root>/.rolepod/parent-active`) and dblab becomes the DB evidence/gate provider.

interface.shortDescription: Data-layer safety + truth verifier — introspect, assert state, EXPLAIN, schema-drift, guarded writes.
interface.longDescription: rolepod-dblab ships an MCP server with 5 tools (rolepod_db_introspect, rolepod_db_query, rolepod_db_explain, rolepod_db_migrate_verify, rolepod_db_write) and 5 user-invocable skills. It gives a coding agent eyes + safe hands on a live Postgres: prove a migration applied, assert DB state as verify-evidence, read query plans, detect schema drift between SQLAlchemy models and the live DB, and mutate data behind a transaction preview→confirm→commit/rollback guard. It is NOT a generic SQL client. Pair with the `rolepod` parent plugin (detected via the marker file `<git-root>/.rolepod/parent-active`) and evidence routes to `<git-root>/.rolepod/evidence/` with a `manifest.json` per Extension Protocol v1. Postgres-only in v1.
defaultPrompt: Use /db-query to assert a row exists with the expected value after my change.
Use /db-migrate-verify to check the live schema against my SQLAlchemy models before shipping.
Use /db-write to apply a data fix safely behind a transaction preview → confirm guard.
